crime.value: Multiple infanticide - bodies of newborn babies found buried in bog, with additional remains in fireplace and furnace
crime.quote: These are modern and they are the bodies of baby's. They appear to have been new born

victim.value: Multiple newborn infants, likely born to unwed mothers during wartime when such pregnancies carried social stigma
victim.quote: during those years a woman being pregnant out of wed lock was looked upon as a lost woman

closed_world.value: The village of Hesterton and Rose Moss Bog area, with a limited group of suspects connected to wartime events
closed_world.quote: I need you to come up here... Up to Hesterton?

culprit_and_motive.value: Anthony Manno (possibly with Charles Fields) - motive appears to be eliminating evidence of illegitimate births during wartime
culprit_and_motive.quote: That leaves Anthony Manno... Or Charles Fields

resolution.value: Basham provides investigative profile and theory to Inspector Godwin, recommends questioning Elizabeth Garrick for cooperation
resolution.quote: I suggest you question Elizabeth Garrick and soon before she dies and takes many answers with her

investigator.value: Alistair Basham (consulting detective/profiler) working with Inspector Godwin and archaeologist Joyce
investigator.quote: Alistair Basham sat in his favorite chair in his study

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects with differentiated motives: Anthony Manno, Charles Fields, Elizabeth Garrick (ruled out due to physical inability), Margaret Ainsworth. Suspects span family members, associates, with motives ranging from covering up infanticide to protecting family secrets.
suspect_architecture.quote: That leaves Anthony Manno,' said Basham. ' Or Charles Fields,' said Godwin.

red_herring.value: The initial assumption that the bog bodies were ancient archaeological finds (neolithic or Roman period) when they were actually modern infant murders, deliberately misdirecting from contemporary crime to historical discovery.
red_herring.quote: Because the bodies are not from the neolithic or Roman period. These are modern and they are the bodies of baby's.

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence presented to reader simultaneously with investigators: eyeglasses frames identified by witness, skeletal remains indicating victim height, evidence from fireplace and furnace all described as discovered.
clue_fairness.quote: Inspector Godwin removed the silver frame containing one lens from a paper bag and handed it to the woman.

social_world.value: WWII-era social stigma around unmarried pregnancy creating conditions for infanticide. Class dynamics between family members, wartime displacement, and social shame driving criminal behavior and cover-ups.
social_world.quote: during those years a woman being pregnant out of wed lock was looked upon as a lost woman. She would have been seen as a whore

alibi.value: Competing theories about Edna's fate: married the American soldier and went to America versus local murder. Elizabeth Garrick's physical limitations provide natural alibi against physical assault charges.
alibi.quote: Some believe that she married the Yank and he sent her to America. I doubt that very much

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical evidence analysis through optometrist records for eyeglasses identification, skeletal height measurements to eliminate suspects, and archaeological/forensic methods to distinguish modern from ancient remains.
reveal_mechanic.quote: I can tell by the frames. I was with her at the optometrist shop when she had her examination.

investigator_wound.value: Basham's academic detachment conflicting with personal involvement as he must provide profile and theory but withdraw before final resolution, leaving unresolved questions about unknown victim's identity.
investigator_wound.quote: We probably may never know who that unfortunate woman was. Perhaps you will learn who she is as you continue

_meta.title: ebook robert f clifton the case of the bog bodies
